Landschaftspflegeverein

33314415-10e1-817d-bb61-c182a27a3f84, 33314415-10e1-8115-acda-c46330326a6d, 33314415-10e1-8165-94c8-deb3330b53c8, 33314415-10e1-810e-b19c-e648bfbfe187
network_affiliation
[[Bioregional Weaving Labs]]
background_section
## Background The Thermenlinie Wiener Becken—south of Vienna—is easy to read as sprawl, intensive farms, and busy roads. Under that surface sit major biological hotspots. The belt is [among Europe’s most species-rich landscapes](https://youtu.be/k5OiXPqz8pM): former pastures such as Bertholdsdorf Heide, Güberheide, and Symposium Linderbrunn, plus scattered wetlands. Alps meet Pannonian lowlands here, so flora and fauna blend alpine and steppic elements. Many hotspots are **grasslands**—dry meadows, old pastures, wet meadows, and fens—holding rare and protected species. Dry grasslands are especially rich in flowers and insects; wet or dry, grasslands lock up large soil carbon stocks. [Globally, grasslands hold roughly 30% of carbon stored in biomass](https://youtu.be/k5OiXPqz8pM)—important for climate mitigation. Without large wild grazers (aurochs, wisent long gone), these open habitats now depend on continued human care; left alone, they scrub over and succeed to forest—and much of the open-habitat diversity vanishes. ### Origins and Network Founded **June 2017**, the **Landschaftspflegeverein** is a non-profit devoted to long-term biodiversity in the Thermenlinie–Wienerwald–Wiener Becken corridor. Work began with a practical question: how to manage greenspaces differently from routine frequent mowing—what would be sustainable? The aim is to rekindle local enthusiasm for “nature next door.”

Place
Austria
place
Thermenlinie Wiener Becken, Austria
activities
Network Naturregion Building|Nature Tours and Experiences|School Education Programs|Sustainable Grassland Management
description
Works to preserve and restore one of the most species-rich regions in Europe through network building, community involvement, and sustainable grassland management. They connect people and organizations to create a network of natural areas.
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy **Network Naturregion** keeps widening—people and parcels woven together. Modest changes such as less frequent mowing have already yielded plant and insect assemblages many residents no longer recognise from childhood. Grasslands remain carbon-rich and biologically extraordinary; maintaining the mosaic still depends on many hands—volunteers, educators, farmers, and public partners sharing the load.
website
https://landschaftspflegeverein.at/
bioregional_context
The Thermenlinie Wiener Becken (Vienna Basin Thermal Line) region is one of the most species-rich regions in Europe, featuring former pasture areas and wetlands. The region's exceptional biodiversity comes from its position between the Alps and the Pannonian lowlands, hosting species from both areas. Grasslands store about 30% of global carbon in biomass.
category
